notes: This could be either a 0 or 1 I think. A is more assertive here but not because he is trying to be, that may just be their natural dynamic. So I did balanced because this interaction seems balanced to me.
Borderline between -1 and -2 since she tries to push back a few times. Overall, Charlie is much more in control though.
Starts with A being more assertive but barely since B is pushing back. Then it shifts to B being much more assertive, leading to overall B being slightly more assertive.
This one is almost balanced, but Josh pulls rank here and directs the flow of conversation in addition to ordering Donna.
Abbey is very dominant here, but Reeseman pushes back enough to make it slightly more balanced.
The prior conversation about alcoholism is not relevant to the conversation in this document which is balanced.
Although Josh is certainly on the defensive here and in a weaker position, in this conversation, Bartlet doesn't act assertive or push it until the end.
This is balanced overall since neither is being particularly assertive and it is fairly equal. But there is a power shift since Donna starts by making fun of Josh and then he dismisses her.
This is a great example of clashing being balanced. They are both being very assertive. It switches from Mandy to Josh though in the middle.
This one is tricky because of lack of context, but Toby gets angry and reprimands Mary, gaining some authority.
Pretty balanced overall, starts with Toby having the upper hand and then switches
Toby doesn't seem to be trying to take control, but Sam readily gives it to him.
